metadata.summary: A clean book theme for scientific explanations and documentation with Sphinx

documentation:
# sphinx-book-theme

[![codecov][codecov-badge]][codecov-link] [![Documentation Status][rtd-badge]][rtd-link] [![PyPI][pypi-badge]][pypi-link]

**An interactive book theme for Sphinx**.

This is a lightweight Sphinx theme designed to mimic the look-and-feel of an
interactive book. It has the following primary features:

* **Bootstrap 5**
  for visual elements and functionality.
* **Flexible content layout** that is inspired by beautiful online books,
  such as [the Edward Tufte CSS guide](https://edwardtufte.github.io/tufte-css/)
* **Visual classes designed for Jupyter Notebooks**. Cell inputs, outputs,
  and interactive functionality are all supported.
* **Launch buttons for online interactivity**. For pages that are built with
  computational material, connect your site to an online BinderHub for interactive content.

## Get started

To get started with `sphinx-book-theme`, first install it with `pip`:

```
pip install sphinx-book-theme
```

then, activate the theme in your Sphinx configuration (`conf.py`):

```
...
html_theme = "sphinx_book_theme"
...
```

This will activate the Sphinx Book Theme for your documentation. Note that you may
need to change your `html_theme_options` configuration depending on your previous
theme. See the pages to the left for information about what you can configure with
`sphinx-book-theme`.

## Documentation

See [the Sphinx Book Theme documentation](https://sphinx-book-theme.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
for more information.

[codecov-badge]: https://codecov.io/gh/executablebooks/sphinx-book-theme/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
[codecov-link]: https://codecov.io/gh/executablebooks/sphinx-book-theme

[rtd-badge]: https://readthedocs.org/projects/sphinx-book-theme/badge/?version=latest
[rtd-link]: https://sphinx-book-theme.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest

[pypi-badge]: https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/sphinx-book-theme.svg
[pypi-link]: https://pypi.org/project/sphinx-book-theme

classes.name: sphinx_book_theme.nodes.SideNoteNode
documentation:
Handles rendering of side/marginnote content text for html outputs.
Inserts required html to handle both desktop and mobile.

modules.name: sphinx_book_theme._compile_translations
documentation: Generate compiled static translation assets for Sphinx.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme._compile_translations.convert_json
documentation:
Convert JSON translations into .mo/.po files for Sphinx.
folder:
    the source folder of the JSON translations. This function will put the
    compiled .mo/.po files in a specific folder relative to this source
    folder. This parameter is just provided to make testing easier.

modules.name: sphinx_book_theme
documentation: A lightweight book theme based on the pydata sphinx theme.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.get_html_theme_path
documentation: Return list of HTML theme paths.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.add_metadata_to_page
documentation: Adds some metadata about the page that we reuse later.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.hash_assets_for_files
documentation:
Generate a hash for assets, and append to its entry in context.

assets: a list of assets to hash, each path should be relative to
     the theme's static folder.

theme_static: a path to the theme's static folder.

context: the Sphinx context object where asset links are stored. These are:
    `css_files` and `script_files` keys.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.hash_html_assets
documentation:
Add ?digest={hash} to assets in order to bust cache when changes are made.

The source files are in `static` while the built HTML is in `_static`.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.update_mode_thebe_config
documentation: Update thebe configuration with SBT-specific values

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.check_deprecation_keys
documentation: Warns about the deprecated keys.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.update_templates
documentation:
Update template names and assets for page build.

This is a copy of what the pydata theme does here to include a new section
- https://github.com/pydata/pydata-sphinx-theme/blob/0a4894fab49befc59eb497811949a1d0ede626eb/src/pydata_sphinx_theme/__init__.py#L173 # noqa: E501

variables.name: sphinx_book_theme.__version__
documentation: sphinx-book-theme version

modules.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons
documentation: Generate metadata for header buttons.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.as_bool
documentation:
Cast string as a boolean with some extra checks.

If var is a string, it will be matched to 'true'/'false'
If var is a bool, it will be returned
If var is None, it will return False.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.get_repo_parts
documentation: Return the parts of the source repository.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.get_repo_url
documentation: Return the provider URL based on what is defined in context.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.prep_header_buttons
documentation: Prep an empty list that we'll populate with header buttons.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.add_header_buttons
documentation: Add basic and general header buttons, we'll add source/launch later.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.update_context_with_repository_info
documentation:
Update pydata `html_context` options for source from `repository_url`.

We do this because we use repository_url as one config to define the URL,
while the PST uses a collection of {provider}_{key} pairs in html_context.
So here we insert those context variables on our own.

modules.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.launch
documentation: Launch buttons for Binder / Thebe / Colab / etc.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.launch.add_launch_buttons
documentation:
Builds a binder link and inserts it in HTML context for use in templating.

This is a `html-page-context` sphinx event (see {ref}`sphinx:events`).

```
param pagename

The sphinx docname related to the page

param context

A dictionary of values that are given to the template engine,
to render the page and can be modified to include custom values.

param doctree

A doctree when the page is created from a reST documents;
it will be None when the page is created from an HTML template alone.
```

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.launch._split_repo_url
documentation: Split a repository URL into an org / repo combination.

classes.name: sphinx_book_theme.directives.Margin

methods.name: sphinx_book_theme.directives.Margin.run
documentation: Run the directive.
Goes in the margin to the right of the page.

modules.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.source
documentation: Source file buttons that point to the online repository.

functions.name: sphinx_book_theme.header_buttons.source.add_source_buttons
documentation: Add the source repository buttons.

classes.name: sphinx_book_theme._transforms.HandleFootnoteTransform
documentation: Transform footnotes into side/marginnotes.
